purpose: This commit fixes a bug in the Bluetooth subsystem by preventing commands from being queued when HCI_UNREGISTER is set.
The commit removes an unnecessary header file inclusion (asm/termios.h) in a driver file (n_hdlc.c).
The commit adds a new metric 'N/A' to the store operations report table in the perf-c2c tool documentation.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping.
The commit adds two new supply entries to the realtek,rt5682s binding file for AVDD and MICVDD.
The commit fixes a resource leak in zorro7xx_remove_one() by adding an iounmap() call to release the mapped memory.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the Linux kernel's net/mlx5e driver.
The commit changes the type of key_idx from u8 to s8 in cfg80211_auth_request struct.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V architecture.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
This commit adds a new feature to the Linux kernel, specifically for managing private anonymous VMAs.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to a variable that is never read.
This commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks, allowing for a more modular kernel configuration.
The commit removes duplicated text in a comment, which does not affect the functionality of the code.
This commit reverts a change that was made to populate the DCF clock table based on DCFCLK states. The reverted code used DCFCLK as the primary source of information for populating the table.
This commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (a.k.a R9A07G054) to the device tree bindings documentation.
The commit modifies several files in the Linux kernel's PCIe driver to use a new data structure for representing PCIe controllers.
The commit fixes a bug in the clang compiler by changing the format specifier for printing an unsigned short value from %#06hx to %#06x.
The commit fixes a bug in the MIPS architecture by casting the upper bound of the branch range to long for signed comparison.
The commit removes a redundant case in SetHwReg8188EU() that calls a function directly.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static to fix sparse warnings introduced while adding errata.
The commit removes unnecessary interrupt flags from display subsystem nodes in device tree bindings.

The commit adds a new field to store names for private anonymous memory in /proc/pid/maps and /proc/pid/smaps, which allows userspace to name anonymous VMAs displayed as [anon:<name>]. The patch also includes a prctl interface and regression test.
The commit adds new SoCs and PMICs to the socinfo driver, which is responsible for providing information about the system-on-chip (SoC) and power management integrated circuit (PMIC).
The commit adds support for all possible chip cuts in the RTL8xxxU driver.
The commit removes a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks in the Ux500 platform.
The commit appears to be related to memory management and initialization for a RISC-V architecture.
The commit adds new functionality to enable QUP and GPI DMA hardware, which is necessary for adding functional I2C nodes later.
The commit fixes a bug in the thermal driver for Renesas RZG2L SoCs by replacing a confusing comment with a more accurate description of the measurement times.
The commit fixes an infinite loop that may occur when releasing resources in the igc driver.
The commit updates the terminology used in the pcm3168a driver to use more modern language for clocking.
The commit removes a hack that was used to support future NFS swap support.
Implements GTP echo response functionality by adding a new function to handle GTP echo requests and responses.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_zero() with cpumask_first_zero(), which is a more efficient way to find the first unset CPU in a cpumask.
The commit removes a call to an IOVAR that is no longer supported in the firmware, which caused a failure message to be displayed on the console after roaming was complete.
This commit adds support for a new platform called adl_rt5682, which uses the alc5682 headset codec without a speaker amplifier.
The commit adds a new feature to io_uring: fail zerocopy sendmsg when unsupported by socket.
The commit adds a new feature to the kernel documentation system by testing for the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it.
The commit adds a new feature to checkpatch.pl that allows macros with complex values to be enclosed in parentheses.
The commit replaces the simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers.
The commit adds a new feature to the Linux kernel, specifically for managing private anonymous VMAs
The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from libata.
This commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank
The commit fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback function by changing the type of the reset_type parameter from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type.

The commit fixes a bug in the device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl by correcting the name of a property from 'reset' to 'resets', and adds an additionalProperties: false constraint to prevent future similar bugs.
The commit modifies a warning flag in the Makefile to silence an array-bounds warning on GCC 12.
The commit changes the way the device is powered on and off by using pm_runtime_resume_and_get instead of pm_runtime_get_sync.
This commit splits out fixed file installation and removal from io_uring to filetable.c, which is where it belongs.
The commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes in the device tree, each with its own power-domain support.
The commit removes a macro that was only used once in the codebase.
